boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single manual file.
Do not widen into Section 3 or later manual sections in this wave.
Treat the manual as a reference compilation, not as binding regulatory text.
Preserve page locators and line references where available, but expect the text layer to remain page-image-backed for exact wording.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No Section 3 pages in this wave.
No later manual sections in this wave.

reviewStandards.referenceManualText: Treat the source as a compiled reference manual and not as learner-facing or promoted material.
reviewStandards.manualCompilation: Treat the front matter and TOC as reference context unless the text itself imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat annual-filings references and state-letter references as documentation context if they appear.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Valuation Manual, regulations, and state guidance as mapped context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title and introductory text as background unless the text imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat each page window as review-only and do not widen it unless later cleanup explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat dense or ambiguous manual wording as requiring human review unless the page image confirms the wording.

validationRequirements: The plan JSON must exist and parse cleanly.
The plan markdown must exist and carry the expected headings.
The dedicated batch-definition file must exist and stay synchronized with the plan.
The planned batches must stay review-only by default.
The source must remain a reference compilation and not promoted.
The source should remain page-image-backed for exact wording where needed.
